Gap Layer: Value architecture, pricing, packaging, promise control, risk reduction, and revenue model layer.
Gap Impact: Offer operators cannot reliably answer who owns intent, what is current, what must happen next, which evidence is authoritative, or how completion is proven.
Root Cause: Domain knowledge exists mainly as DOCX drafts, so it lacks controlled markdown registries, protocols, owners, validation gates, and runtime memory.
Required Solution: Promote intent into the authoritative Offer OS layer with source IDs, owner, governance rule, dependency links, validation gate, dashboard signal, memory update, and version path.
Required Protocols: Offer Intent protocol: trigger -> source evidence -> owner review -> approval/risk check -> execution or handoff -> validation -> memory/version update.
Required Registries: OFFER_MASTER_REGISTRY plus Agency Global Source Registry.
Required Governance: Offer OS Owner / Value Architecture Lead approves ownership, risk class, cadence, and escalation route.
Required Ownership: Offer OS Owner / Value Architecture Lead
Required Orchestration: Route through Offer orchestration and the agency dependency graph: upstream Sector, Client, Finance, Sales, Branding, Management; downstream Marketing, Sales, Client Delivery, Finance, ClientPartner Acquisition.
Required Validation: Entry criteria, source evidence check, owner sign-off, handoff acceptance, and completion gate.
Required Observability: Dashboard status, freshness date, owner review cadence, risk indicator, and gap closure metric.
Required Evolution: Decision log entry, version log entry, quarterly review, and source registry state update.
Required Dependencies: Upstream: Sector, Client, Finance, Sales, Branding, Management. Downstream: Marketing, Sales, Client Delivery, Finance, ClientPartner Acquisition.
Required Sequencing: 1. Confirm source inventory; 2. create canonical markdown; 3. assign owner/RACI; 4. register dependencies and handoffs; 5. define validation and dashboard signals; 6. approve, version, and schedule evolution review.
Completion Criteria: Offer Intent is complete when the markdown exists at the recommended placement, has an owner and cadence, references source IDs, is registered in the local and agency registry, defines validation and observability, and has a version/evolution log entry.
